question.text: Under SEBI IA Regulations, the fee limits prescribed for individual/HUF non-accredited clients apply to which category of investors?

answer.text: All clients regardless of their accreditation status
Only individual and HUF clients who are non-accredited

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Under SEBI IA Regulations 2013, the prescribed fee limits apply exclusively to individual and HUF clients who are non-accredited. Accredited investors (individuals meeting the accreditation criteria) and non-individual clients (corporates, trusts, etc.) are not subject to these fee caps — the fee can be mutually agreed upon.
**The Trap:** Candidates often assume fee limits apply universally to all clients. The regulation specifically carves out this protection for retail (non-accredited individual/HUF) investors, recognizing that accredited and institutional clients can negotiate on their own.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Accredited investor concepts are tested in NISM Series X-A and also in Series XIX-A (AIF) where minimum investment thresholds vary by accreditation status.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Document each client's accreditation status clearly. For non-accredited individual/HUF clients, ensure your fee structure complies with prescribed limits. For accredited clients, negotiate freely but always ensure fee agreements are in writing.
